frontmatter.title: How society is being shaped by technology

speakers.name: Daniel Catalão
speakers.bio: Daniel Catalão é jornalista/apresentador da RTP, especializado em novas tecnologias e internet e autor do programa TecNet. É professor na Universidade Lusófona do Porto (Computação Multimedia e Interatividade; Técnicas de Expressão Radiofónica) e na Escola Superior de Comunicação Administração e Turismo do Instituto Politécnico de Bragança (Comunicação Digital e Redes Sociais; Cibercultura).

frontmatter.title: Graphics Technology Evolution During the Current Generation of Consoles

speakers.name: Peter Sikachev
speakers.bio: Peter Sikachev has been in the game development industry since 2011. He has since worked on such titles as Rise of the Tomb Raider, Deus Ex: Mankind Divided, Red Dead Redemption 2 and others. Peter is currently employed as a senior graphics programmer at CD PROJEKT RED, working on Cyberpunk 2077. Peter is a guest speaker at Talk a Bit 2020 and he is going to talk a bit about “Graphics Technology Evolution During the Current Generation of Consoles”, where we will discuss key game graphics technologies that became industry standard during the current generation of consoles (PlayStation 4, Xbox One).

frontmatter.title: The past, present and future of Privacy

speakers.name: André Lago
speakers.bio: André is a Software Engineer and has been working on some of Google's User-Facing Privacy products for over a year, being the Technical Lead for both Activity Controls and Google Dashboard. André is a guest speaker at Talk a Bit 2020 and he is going to talk a bit about “The past, present and future of Privacy”. Privacy is an extremely important topic, assuming a crucial role in the design and develop of many products. During this talk, we'll go over the history of Privacy at Google and what its future looks like.

frontmatter.title: The Challenges of Software Engineering in F1

speakers.name: João Neto
speakers.bio: João Neto is working at Mercedes AMG F1, six-time double Formula 1 champions, and is part of the Performance Engineering department as a Software Engineer. An Integrated Masters' in Informatics Engineering and Computation student between 2012 and 2017, joined Doctoral Program in Informatics Engineering for a year before going to the United Kingdom, where he now resides. João is a guest speaker at Talk a Bit 2020 and he is going to talk a bit about "The Challenges of Software Engineering in F1"
